primary_sources.finding: Verification security depends on precise actor identity constraints; GitHub recommends repository plus signer workflow identity, supports exact source digest and predicate type constraints, emits machine-readable JSON, and can deny self-hosted runners.
Artifact verification requires policy evaluation over provenance and expected build identity rather than accepting provenance structure alone.

finding: A cryptographic receipt must not merely record a free-form verifier command. The command itself should be constructed and assessed under a deny-by-default policy requiring exact repository, signer workflow, immutable source commit, predicate type, JSON output, and rejection of self-hosted runners for this trust profile.
design_implication: Cloudflare and Vercel evidence workflows should generate gh attestation verify arguments through this module before invoking the CLI, then pass only accepted verification output into the R-005 cryptographic boundary receipt.

cases: mutable or malformed source identity rejected
signer workflow outside repository path rejected

falsification_route: Demonstrate that the builder accepts an invocation lacking any required identity constraint, accepts a mutable source ref instead of a 40-character commit SHA, or accepts a signer workflow outside the expected repository workflow path.
trust_limit: This policy constrains verifier invocation inputs. It does not prove workflow-controlled predicate content, deployment success, scientific validity, medical truth, or peer execution.
